rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANTs WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. AS TRUSTEE FOR OPTION ONE, HOMEWARD RESIDENTIAL, INC., SAND CANYON CORP. F/K/A OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORP.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Continued to November 1, 2012 to give the moving party an opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GENOVA et al VS. CSRWARE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Discovery And Sanctions Order And Motion To Set Aside Such Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT CSRWARE, INC., KAREN ALONARDO Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Discovery And Sanctions Order And Motion To Set Aside Such Order Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Deny the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement And Appoint A Referee And For Costs And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs PAUL WERMER, CAROL BROWNSON's motion To Enforce Settlement And Appoint A Referee And For Costs And Attorneys' Fees. The motion is continued to December 3, 2012, to be heard by Judge Kahn. The parties are to continue to meet and confer in good faith regarding the construction. Plaintiff may file a supplemental brief by November 16, 2012, and identify any outstanding controversies between the parties. Defendant may file a supplemental opposition by November 27, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the plaintiff's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 20, OTHER party STEVEN HILL's Application To File Amicus Brief. Grant. The brief is not relevant to the C.C.P § 1008 issue before the Court, but the filing is permitted as a matter of courtesy. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MONA FIELD et al VS. DEBRA BOWEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 20, PLAINTIFFs MONA FIELD, RICHARD WINGER, STEPHEN CHESSIN, JENNIFER WOZNIAK, JEFF MACKLER, RODNEY MARTIN's Motion For Reconsideration. The motion is denied. Plaintiff's motion does not comply with CCP § 1008. Plaintiff fails to cite any new law, facts, or circumstances that warrant reconsideration of the Court's prior order. Judge Karnow will hear this motion for reconsideration of his earlier ruling, and does pursuant to the continuation rule despite any earlier 170.6 motion. E.g. Jacobs v. Superior Court, 53 Cal.2d 187, 190 (1959). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied as to Defendants Chen and Salmon and off calendar as moot as to Defendant Kea. As to Defendants Chen and Salmon, Defendants shifted the burden of proof but Plaintiff has raised a triable issue. Defendants' objection to the Stein Declaration is overruled. The Court notes that similar language regarding expert opinion on causation is found in the expert declarations of both sides. As to Defendant Kea, that Defendant was dismissed on October 17, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the plaintiff's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ZOMBIE INC. v. GREE INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Zombie Inc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, Plaintiff Zombie Inc's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel are to appear and advise the court whether the matters at issue have been resolved. If the issues have not been resolved, the court will issue a ruling after the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Atty'S Fees; Req For Judcial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT BRADLEY WHITE Motion For Attorney's fees. Granted. Attorney fees of $ 1,282.00 are awarded in favor of Cross-Defendant White. Cross-Complainants cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing on the motion. Ninety-nine hours for the motion at issue, which involved legal research and no discovery, is exorbitant, including as it does three attorneys and the time spent among the three of them in communications; and in any event the time is, as a whole, unreasonable. The Court has divided that time in half, for 45 hours. Dividing the time spent by 1/5 is not an accurate estimate of the benefit of the underlying motion which in effect excluded one of five causes of action. There is no substantial evidence of time spent on the one cause of action at issue, and given the pendency of the other four claims, the benefit of excising the one claim here (indemnity) is relatively low. The Court estimates one tenth of the benefit to be the best estimate given the record here. Accordingly the fairest estimate for fees here is 45 hrs at the highest billed rate ($285) divided by 10 = 1282. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Atty'S Fees; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT BRADLEY WHITE's motion For Attorney'S Fees. Denied. The court granted Cross-Complainants discretionary relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) from their failure to timely file opposition to the anti-SLAPP motion. This does not warrant attorney fees under the mandatory relief provisions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BARBARA L ONEILL VS. EDUCATION MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, A PENNSYLVANIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And To Form Interrogatories (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 5, DEFENDANT EDUCATION MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'S Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And To Form Interrogatories (Set One) Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Deny the motion without prejudice, pending receipt of the court's courtesy copies of the motion papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: RACHAEL HORLAK VS. KIMPTON HOTELS AND RESTAURANTS GROUP LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT GEORGE SANTORA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the 4th cause of action for constructive discharge is sustained without leave to amend. That cause of action is actually a claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Plaintiff cannot allege that claim against defendant Santora because the parties do not share an employer-employee relationship. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress ("NIED") is overruled. The Court recognizes that NIED is not a distinct cause of action. Under CCP 452, the Court liberally reads the claim as a cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the existence of a duty pursuant to the common law and FEHA. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint does not plead a statutory or constitutional basis for the release of the tissue sample. Cases putatively involving fundamental rights in the context of parental rights are not authority that any such right exists for alleged offspring seeking to identify parents. In addition, releasing the sample would run afoul of HIPPA and CMIA. The Court suggests that plaintiff attempt to contact John Doe's heir/personal representative to obtain access to the information. See CC 56.11(c)(4). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
